article_meta.title: Former President Donald Trump speaks at a campaign rally on April 27, 2023, in Manchester, New Hampshire

reasoning: One clear attribution violation (flagging quoted Trump language) and several missed instances of biased author prose. Some identified items are defensible, but labeling is a bit off on one.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: The model improperly flagged a direct Trump quote (“Can you believe? This is what we’re doing.”), violating the attribution rule.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Of three items, one is clearly wrong (quoted text). The other two are defensible as author-level bias.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: Missed multiple notable author-prose biases: “dramatic story,” “late Cuban despot Fidel Castro,” “vague and unverified claim,” and “people should treat his story with great skepticism.”
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: “Added more color” as Subjective Qualifying Adjectives is reasonable; “no reason to believe him” labeled as Word Choice is debatable but not egregiously wrong.
